human_verification.notes: Verified customs-specific scope, research source coverage, classification and valuation review boundaries, escalation/refusal behavior, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit customs review, signoff refusal, fraud escalation, and remediation tracking behavior.
All required abilities across shared, review-assessment-and-inspection, and financial-services-and-insurance layers are fully covered.

specialist_owned_evidence: Classification and valuation review outputs are tied to named customs records, rulings, and valuation artifacts.
The lane refuses final legal or licensed signoff and escalates when fraud, authority conflicts, or missing evidence materially change the result.
Remediation, resubmission, reconciliation, and post-entry follow-up handling is explicit rather than implied.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim in the package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after constraining the lane to U.S. customs review support and explicit escalation boundaries.
Pack is suitable for validated spec-pack deployment and can be promoted to a retained-implementation runtime only through a separate explicit build decision.
